title: Maneuvering
summary: Slow regrouping for a winning setup

steps.title: The Art of Doing Nothing
steps.text: In closed positions, progress is made by slow regrouping — moving knights on winding paths (like Nc3-e2-g3-f5), rerouting bishops, shifting queens. Every move improves a piece by a fraction.

steps.title: Karpov's Way
steps.text: Anatoly Karpov was the master of maneuvering. He would spend 30 moves improving piece placement without apparent threats, then strike with crushing precision. Study his games to learn patience.
